demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged care facility, nursing staff on site

contact_nok.relationship: residential aged care nursing staff (present at the call)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: One minute my heart is pounding out of my chest, and the next I feel so incredibly weak and dizzy that I just black out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The resident fainted today while walking to the bathroom and was out for a couple of minutes; over the last two days her pulse has been swinging between very fast and very slow, and she is now too weak to get up.

symptom_narrative.onset: about three months of steadily worsening tiredness and weakness, then a sharp change over the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: 3 months of fatigue; much worse in the last 2 days; fainted today
symptom_narrative.character: heart races and pounds, then slows right down and she goes weak, grey and dizzy and blacks out
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst weakness she has ever had — she blacked out completely and is now too weak to stand
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: a whole-body weakness with the pounding felt in the chest
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes and goes unpredictably — bursts of a racing heart, then long slow spells
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up or trying to walk
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: palpitations during the fast spells, feeling lightheaded when she stands, breathless with any effort, profound fatigue
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: increasing dizzy spells over the past months but no full blackout until today
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: now bedbound — cannot walk to the bathroom without collapsing, needs staff for everything

past_medical_history.how_managed: recently started on a new blood pressure tablet

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: metoprolol (a new blood pressure tablet, low dose, started recently)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged care facility; high falls risk; nursing staff are monitoring her at the bedside

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single facility-cuff reading taken by nursing staff, not continuous
manual/observed pulse; rhythm not assessable without an ECG

objective_data_offered.value: over the last 48 hours staff have counted the pulse as low as about 40 and as high as about 130 at different times
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: intermittent spot pulse counts by staff, not a monitored trace

objective_data_offered.value: frail, pale, drowsy and slow to answer, lying flat in bed
bed rails up and a fall mat placed beside the bed by staff
